findings: The implementation adds the scoped exit module, rule/policy contracts, engine parameters, exports, and targeted tests for exit-rule handling within the per-symbol backtest engine.
Deterministic single-close behavior is implemented when triggers fire: the engine selects one trigger via the close policy and replaces same-step intents with exactly one full-position sell intent.

The approved spec is not satisfied for holding-period semantics: OpenPositionState does not track holding_bar_count, and MaxHoldingPeriodRule uses calendar-day difference from opened_at instead of bar-backed holding_bar_count.

The approved spec is not satisfied for price-trigger semantics: StopLossRule evaluates against bar.low and TakeProfitRule evaluates against bar.high, while the spec requires inclusive comparisons using current bar.close against average entry price.

The approved spec is not satisfied for no-trigger behavior when exit rules are enabled: the engine pre-filters intents to buy-only before trigger evaluation, so raw intents are not executed unchanged when no trigger fires.

The flat-position behavior is not preserved under configured exit rules: raw sell intents are filtered out even when the engine is flat, which violates the requirement to preserve current engine behavior for flat raw sells.

Test coverage is incomplete relative to the approved spec because the current tests do not enforce bar-backed holding_bar_count behavior, close-price stop-loss/take-profit semantics, or unchanged raw-intent execution when exit rules are configured but no triggers fire.
failed_criteria: MaxHoldingPeriodRule must use bar-backed holding_bar_count rather than raw calendar-day difference.
Internal open-position state must support holding_bar_count semantics for open long positions.
StopLossRule must use current bar.close against average entry price with inclusive threshold comparison.
TakeProfitRule must use current bar.close against average entry price with inclusive threshold comparison.
If exit rules are configured and no triggers fire, the engine must execute raw intents unchanged.
If the engine is flat, raw sell signals must preserve current engine behavior even when exit rules are configured.
Tests must cover the missing rule semantics and backward-compatibility cases above.
required_fixes: Extend the open-position state and engine bookkeeping to maintain holding_bar_count for open long positions and evaluate MaxHoldingPeriodRule from that bar-backed count.
Change StopLossRule and TakeProfitRule to compare the current bar.close against the position's average entry price using inclusive threshold checks.
Adjust SymbolBacktestEngine intent handling so raw intents remain unchanged unless an exit trigger is actually selected; do not pre-filter to buy-only before confirming a close decision.
Preserve existing flat-position raw sell behavior when exit rules are enabled.

Add tests that lock in holding_bar_count semantics, close-based stop-loss/take-profit behavior, no-trigger raw-intent pass-through, and flat-position backward compatibility under configured exit rules.
summary: Static validation fails because several required T14 behaviors are implemented incorrectly: holding-period logic uses calendar days instead of holding bars, stop-loss and take-profit use intrabar extremes instead of close price, and enabling exit rules changes raw-intent behavior even when no trigger fires or the engine is flat.
